channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Thousands of Rhode Island citizens have data stolen after social services hit by cyberattack
item.description: RIBridges benefits system hit by a cyberattack that likely compromised the PII of thousands of Rhode Island citizens.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: RCS encryption is still months away following major US telecomms breach
item.description: After the FBI warned of a major security breach, the world’s leading mobile networks industry organization is spearheading efforts to bring encryption to RCS – but it’s going to take a while to get there.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: La Consejería de Salud de Cantabria, galardonada en los ‘CIO 100 Awards’ como Administración Pública del Año
item.description: La Consejería de Salud de Cantabria ha sido reconocida como Administración Pública del Año en el marco de la celebración de los CIO 100 Awards Spain 2024.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Schools are cutting bus service for children. Parents are turning to ride-hailing apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Mcmurray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Thrive Capital’s Vince Hankes is betting big on OpenAI
item.description: “We’re in this massive paradigm shift,” says Vince Hankes, the venture capitalist leading Thrive Capital’s investment in OpenAI. “And when that happens, historically, there have been new companies created that are worth a lot of money.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: If Congress won’t regulate AI, Scott Wiener believes California must
item.description: Last summer, Scott Wiener found himself in an uncomfortable position for any California Democrat: openly quarreling with Nancy Pelosi and garnering support from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: For December’s Patch Tuesday, 74 updates and a zero-day fix for Windows
item.description: Microsoft released 74 updates in its December Patch Tuesday update, with patches for Windows, Office and Edge — but none for Microsoft Exchange Server or SQL server.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Warning: This fake PayPal email scam is everywhere right now
item.description: Fraudsters are sending people bogus invoices through PayPal as part of a sneaky scam that is going around; here's how to protect yourself from being fooled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NotebookLM Plus is now available to Google Workspace customers
item.description: A new premium version of Google’s NotebookLM AI assistant is now available to Google Workspace customers. Google unveiled NotebookLM last year, initially under the name Project Tailwind, and began testing the AI-powered “notebook” with select users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: Sophia, a world-renowned robot, has been the center of attention at an Artificial Intelligence and Innovation fair in Zimbabwe this week.
item.dc:creator: By Farai Mutsaka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thiel says he won't have 'full-time' role in second Trump administration
item.description: Tech billionaire Peter Thiel said he won’t have a "full-time" role in the second President-elect Trump administration, adding that he would become “depressed and crazy” if he spent his “whole life” thinking about politics.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 18 indispensable Android travel apps
item.description: For all the ways travel’s evolved over the years, one thing has remained maddeningly steadfast: Moving from one place to another is almost always a hassle. There’s endless room for inconvenience and error, and a journey rarely goes according to plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Discover’s hybrid cloud journey pays off
item.description: Discover Financial Services has moved aggressively to the cloud in 2024 with a migration strategy focused on retaining hybrid flexibility and making the most of cloud elasticity.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is data science? Transforming data into value
item.description: What is data science? Data science is a method to glean insights from structured and unstructured data using approaches ranging from statistical analysis to machine learning (ML).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Scale AI sued by former worker alleging unlawful business practices
item.description: A new class action lawsuit alleges poor working conditions and exploitive behavior by AI data processing company Scale AI, saying that workers responsible for generating much of its product were mischaracterized by the company as independent contractors, rather than full employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to keep your holiday shopping a secret on Amazon
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to keep your online Amazon gift purchases a secret from loved ones or friends this holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AWS adds Graviton Savings Dashboard to help enterprises optimize infra costs
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has launched a dashboard for its Arm-based Graviton CPU that will measure savings opportunities in an effort to help enterprises further optimize their expenditure on subscribed infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Network World’s Best of Enterprise Network 2024 award winners
item.description: In many ways, networking is a mature, stable industry, based on established technologies like Wi-Fi and Ethernet, which just celebrated its 50th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows iPhone users how to easily delete multiple emails and manage their inboxes to reduce digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Energy prioritizes information sharing, AI for federated enterprise cybersecurity
item.description: The federated nature of the Energy Department's enterprise requires a focus on information sharing in order to identify and prevent cybersecurity events. The post Energy prioritizes information sharing, AI for federated enterprise cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network
